findings.impact: Remote attackers can attempt direct privileged logins instead of going through named accounts.
findings.remediation: Set PermitRootLogin no in sshd_config and reload sshd.
findings.title: SSH root login is permitted

findings.impact: Password-based SSH increases exposure to credential stuffing and weak password attacks.
findings.remediation: Prefer key-based SSH and set PasswordAuthentication no after confirming access.
findings.title: SSH password authentication is enabled

findings.impact: Memory corruption exploits become easier when process address layouts are predictable.
findings.remediation: Set kernel.randomize_va_space=2 with sysctl and persist it under /etc/sysctl.d/.
findings.title: Address space layout randomization is disabled

findings.impact: The host can route traffic between interfaces, which broadens blast radius if unintended.
findings.remediation: Set net.ipv4.ip_forward=0 unless this system is intentionally acting as a router.
findings.title: IPv4 forwarding is enabled

findings.impact: Malicious redirects can influence host routing decisions on hostile networks.
findings.remediation: Set net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_redirects=0 unless redirects are explicitly required.
findings.title: IPv4 ICMP redirects are accepted

findings.impact: Low-address memory mappings can make kernel null-pointer bugs easier to exploit.
findings.remediation: Set vm.mmap_min_addr to at least 65536 unless a legacy workload requires otherwise.
findings.title: Low memory mappings are not protected
